item.title: UTCYD Snyder Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-07-07
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Canyon Country District.
---
State: Utah
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 39° 0 28 Longitude: 109° 3 31
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Snyder Fire started on Saturday, June 26th during a Red Flag Warning with gusty winds, low relative humidity and dry fuel. Engines, crews and aircraft were used during initial suppression efforts.  Due to extreme fire behavior, crews were moved back to staging areas for their safety on Saturday night.  Crews returned to suppression efforts when conditions improved. The Rocky Mountain Complex Incident Management Team 1 assumed command of the incident on Monday morning, June 29.Links to official information for the Snyder Fire can be found at this website: Snyder Fire LinktreeAn interactive map of the Snyder Fire is available on the National Interagency Fire Center GIS database. This map contains the fire perimeter for the Snyder Fire. You can enter a specific location and measure from that location to the edge of the fire.  Pre-evacuations As of July 1, 2026, at 7 p.m. all pre-evacuation and evacuation notices issued in response to the Snyder Fire near Glade Park, Colorado have been lifted by the Mesa County Sheriff. Visit the Sheriff's Facebook page for current status of road closures. Sign up for Emergency Alerts at the Grand Junction Regional Communication Center website Colorado Parks & Wildlife Area Closures related to Snyder Fire can be found at this link: Colorado River closed to all public access and downstream recreation from Fruita to the Utah state line | Colorado Parks and Wildlife Bureau of Land Management Closures related to the Snyder Fire can be found at these links:BLM Revised Snyder Fire Area ClosureUpper Colorado River District Fire RestrictionsGrand Junction Field Office | Bureau of Land Management Fire restrictions in Mesa County, the Grand Mesa, Uncompahgre and Gunnison National Forests and the BLM Grand Junction Field Office can be found at these links:Fire restrictions from Emergency Services | Mesa CountyGrand Mesa, Uncompahgre and Gunnison National Forests | GMUG National Forests Move to Stage 2 Fire Restrictions | Forest ServiceStage 2 Fire Restrictions | Bureau of Land Management A Temporary Flight Restriction has been issued due to the use of firefighting aircraft. Details can be found at this link: Snyder Fire Temporary Flight Restriction. Wildfires are a no-fly zone for unauthorized aircraft, including drones. If you fly, we can’t. For more information, visit http://knowbeforeyoufly.org. Governor Polis declared a State Of Emergency on June 27: Governor Polis Verbally Declares Disaster Emergency and Authorizes National Guard For Snyder Mesa Fire in Mesa County | Colorado Governor Jared Polis
item.dc:creator: rreuse

item.title: NVELD Grapevine
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-07-07
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Ely District.
---
State: Nevada
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 37° 22 51 Longitude: 114° 18 17
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Grapevine Fire is burning in timber in the Clover Mountain Wilderness Area, 10 miles southeast of Caliente, Nevada.
item.dc:creator: jacobchadwick

item.title: UTMLF Babylon Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-07-06
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Manti-Lasal National Forest.
---
State: Utah
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 37° 44 4 Longitude: 109° 48 31
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Babylon Fire, which began on Friday, June 26, 2026, about 25 miles southwest of Monticello, Utah, has grown to 87,554 acres. This significant growth is driven by a combination of gusty winds, low humidity, and dry vegetation across the region. Firefighters are actively working to contain the blaze using a mix of direct suppression and indirect confinement strategies. There are currently 730 personnel assigned to the fire.Due to active firefighting operations from the Babylon Fire, extensive public land closures are now in effect to ensure public and firefighter safety. All Forest Service lands, roads, and trails within the Monticello Ranger District of the Manti-La Sal National Forest are strictly closed, alongside the entire Needles District of Canyonlands National Park. Additionally, the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) has closed all managed lands west of Harts Draw Road, extending north through the Indian Creek Corridor to Indian Creek Falls, which encompasses Shay Mesa, Beef Basin, Dark Canyon, and Sweet Alice. For your safety and the safety of emergency crews, the public is advised to completely avoid the Monticello Ranger District and stay clear of all BLM and Forest Service lands south of Highway 211, west of Highway 191, and north of Highway 95.
item.dc:creator: mfidler

item.title: WAOLP Mount Tom Creek Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-07-07
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Olympic National Park.
---
State: Washington
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 47° 49 20.3988 Longitude: 123° 47 58.8006
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: Situation: The lightning-caused Mt. Tom Creek Fire is burning high on a remote mountain slope on the west side of Olympic National Park. The area is in steep, densely forested terrain approximately five miles west‑northwest of Mount Olympus. Backpackers first reported the fire on June 24th, the morning after a lightning storm swept through the area.The fire is approximately six miles from the Hoh Rain Forest administrative site. The Hoh Rain Forest Visitor Center, campground, and trails all remain open to the public.Public and firefighter safety are the top priority as crews work to suppress this fire. Firefighter rappelers surveyed the area and determined that inserting crews directly from aircraft was not feasible. The fire is too far from potential landing zones and established trails that could serve as evacuation routes, making on‑the‑ground operations risky. Steep slopes, dense vegetation, and standing dead trees add further hazards for firefighters working in this rugged landscape.Though high relative humidity and periodic precipitation may constrain fire behavior, they are unlikely to extinguish the fire.Smoke: Smoke may be visible from various locations on the Olympic Peninsula. Air quality can change quickly.  For current smoke information, visit: https://fire.airnow.govFire Restrictions: Olympic National Park implemented fire restrictions June 25th. Dispersed and backcountry fires are prohibited. Fires within established metal fire rings in developed campgrounds and picnic areas are exempt from this prohibition. Propane and gas stoves are allowed, if operated at least three feet away from flammable vegetation and forest litter.Air Space Restrictions: A Temporary Flight Restriction is in place. If you fly, we can’t. Every time a drone is spotted near the fire all aircraft are grounded until we can be sure the drone is clear of this area: http://knowbeforeyoufly.org
item.dc:creator: jlow

item.title: NMGNF Sacaton Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-07-07
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Gila National Forest.
---
State: New Mexico
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 33° 16 49 Longitude: 108° 40 41
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: Background: On the afternoon of Sunday, June 21, the Mogollon Baldy lookout detected a lightning-caused fire on Sacaton Mountain (elevation 10,658 feet) in the Gila Wilderness. The fire, which is on the Glenwood Ranger District, was initially about 4.5 miles west of the lookout tower. Firefighters quickly conducted an aerial assessment and estimated the fire—burning in brush, timber, logs, and snags—was about 2 acres and exhibiting moderate fire behavior. By late evening, the lookout reported the fire had grown to approximately 4 acres.The fire’s location presents extreme risks to both ground and aerial firefighters: It is burning among numerous snags, a dense tangle of fallen trees, and thick brush regeneration from the 2012 Whitewater-Baldy Fire. The landscape is very rugged and steep (e.g., 40–60 percent slopes). Safety zones and escape routes are nonexistent. Emergency extraction times would be unacceptably long (i.e., 6+ hours) and logistically difficult. High-elevation flying, especially during hot weather, is very hazardous and an unacceptable transfer of risk to the pilot.Smoke & Air Quality: Smoke is visible from numerous areas in and surrounding the Gila National Forest. Scroll down for air quality information and the AirNow Fire and Smoke Map.
item.dc:creator: emacewen

item.title: COPSF Aspen Acres Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-07-07
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Pike and San Isabel National Forest.
---
State: Colorado
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 38° 1 10 Longitude: 105° 4 17
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Aspen Acres Fire was first reported on June 29th at approximately 6:00am that originated 10 miles northwest of Rye, Colorado near the Aspen Acres Campground in the area of the San Isabel National Forest.It experienced explosive growth due to record low fuel moisture levels and persistent red flag conditions in the week prior to and days following the ignition. The fire was wind driven by 40+ mph winds with gusts estimated to be up to 100 mph. It has impacted the community of Beulah, Colorado and the surrounding area in both Custer and Pueblo Counties.
item.dc:creator: ncarpenter

item.title: UTFIF Wild Goose Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-07-07
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Fishlake National Forest.
---
State: Utah
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 39° 6 41.07 Longitude: 112° 10 31.46
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Wild Goose Fire began on June 26, 2026 just south of Wild Goose Canyon on the Fishlake National Forest. It is located east of Holden and south of Scipio, Utah.The fire is burning in continuous heavy dead and down fuels, steep terrain and standing timber fuel types. Fuels are exceptionally dry for this time of year and contributing to extreme fire behavior. Probability of ignition is near 80-100%. The fire grew very quickly in the first 24 hours and is continuing to spread.Great Basin Complex Incident Management Team 7 assumed command of the Wild Goose Fire Sunday, June 28, 2026.
item.dc:creator: lwilkolak

item.title: WYBTF Kinky Creek Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-07-07
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Bridger-Teton National Forest.
---
State: Wyoming
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 43° 25 19.95 Longitude: 110° 11 37.33
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The lightning-caused fire is located west of Darwin Ranch, near upper Gros Ventre Falls in the Gros Ventre Wilderness on the Jackson Ranger District of the Bridger-Teton National Forest, and is burning in heavy timber in steep challenging terrain.Fire managers are implementing direct extinguishment and indirect confinement strategies on the Kinky Creek Fire. A direct extinguishment strategy is when firefighters engage the active flame front directly to stop fire spread. An indirect confinement strategy is when firefighters work away from the active flame front to steer or contain the fire.The public is asked to avoid Kinky Creek Road; Kinky Creek and Clear Creek trails; and Brewster, Lunch, and Chateau lakes so fire crews can work safely and efficiently. The forest has issued a closure order.The fire danger rating is currently “high” for the Teton Interagency Dispatch area, which includes Bridger-Teton National Forest, Grand Teton National Park, and the National Elk Refuge.For other news, events and information, visit the Bridger-Teton National Forest website and follow the Bridger-Teton on Facebook and X @BridgerTetonNF.
item.dc:creator: cadams3

item.title: AZCOF Pocket Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-07-06
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Coconino National Forest.
---
State: Arizona
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 34° 58 1.9632 Longitude: 111° 47 14.715
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Pocket Fire, which started on June 19, 2026, approximately 7 miles north of Sedona, Arizona, continues to back and flank through numerous large, steep canyons north of Sedona and along the State Highway 89A corridor. Steep terrain and the extensive canyon system continue to present operational challenges, with rolling material creating a significant risk of fire spread below the main fire edge.Current fire behavior is characterized by backing and flanking fire with short uphill runs occurring where fire aligns with slope reversals or where rolling material ignites receptive fuels. Critically dry fuels remain highly susceptible to ignition, allowing for continued fire spread under favorable conditions.
item.dc:creator: rshaffer

item.title: COGMF Gold Mountain Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-07-06
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Grand Mesa Uncompahgre and Gunnison National Forests.
---
State: Colorado
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 38° 3 6 Longitude: 107° 40 56
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Gold Mountain Fire was discovered on June 27, 2026, at approximately 5:33 pm 2 miles northeast of Ouray, CO. Fire initially started burning in mixed conifer vegetation located on inaccessible terrain above the east side of Highway 550. Local, State, and Federal initial attack fire resources were ordered immediately. The fire spread quickly running up steep slopes. A local Type 3 Incident Management Team was assigned on June 27, 2026. Aviation resources were ordered throughout the next day, but high winds and terrain features shut down the use of aviation resources. Fire became very active, with group torching and crowning, increasing the fire growth to the east by 2,000 acres. Fire resources provided public safety and worked to protect structures. Rocky Mountain Complex Incident Management Team 3 was ordered on June 28, 2026, and took command on June 29, 2026, at 6 pm.
item.dc:creator: clepa

item.title: UTFIF Cottonwood Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-07-06
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Fishlake National Forest.
---
State: Utah
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 38° 16 11 Longitude: 112° 33 3
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Cottonwood Fire started on June 22, 2026 and experienced rapid growth fueled by extremely dry fuels and weather conditions conducive to fire spread. First responders worked to evacuate the fire area on June 23. Great Basin Complex Incident Management Team 5 assumed command of the Cottonwood Fire on June 24, 2026. The fire destroyed several structures and has impacted forest recreation sites. Structures that are still standing have structure protection groups assigned to them to prevent further loss. Firefighters are working to minimize acres burned by constructing direct containment lines along the fire perimeter, and air resources continue to support firefighters on the ground with fire suppression objectives. Weather conditions, dry fuels, steep terrain, and limited access continue to be major factors as crews work to increase containment.Live Air Quality and Smoke information is available on this page if you scroll down below the Incident Info section.
item.dc:creator: jswope

item.title: COSJF Ferris Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-07-06
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) San Juan National Forest.
---
State: Colorado
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 37° 37 45.3 Longitude: 108° 40 15.7
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Ferris Fire is burning approximately 15 miles northwest of Dolores, Colorado on the San Juan National Forest. Lightning was the cause. Strong sustained winds, low relative humidity, and extremely dry fuels are driving fire behavior. Crews are working to contain the fire as quickly and as safely possible but the volatile conditions dictate the actions that they are able to take. Fire severity varies, depending on the fuel type (trees, brush, grass), topography and weather.Evacuations and Closures:  Click this link for an interactive Evacuations Status Map for real-time information for Dolores, Montezuma and San Miguel counties. You can also follow Dolores County Office of Emergency Management for additional details in that county. The San Juan National Forest has issued a Fire Area Closure.
item.dc:creator: jenniferhaney

item.title: NMCAF Beehive Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-07-06
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Carson National Forest.
---
State: New Mexico
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 36° 44 14 Longitude: 106° 09 53
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Beehive Fire was first reported at approximately 1 p.m. on June 26 in the Tusas Valley, north of Highway 64 and about 15 miles west of Tres Piedras, New Mexico. The fire was discovered by the crew of a detection flight that had been dispatched to survey the forest following recent lightning activity across the area.As the fire grew and suppression needs increased, management of the incident was transitioned to Southwest Incident Management Team 3, led by Incident Commander Steven Griego. The team is coordinating firefighting resources and working closely with local, state, and federal partners to implement strategies that prioritize firefighter and public safety while protecting natural and cultural resources and minimizing impacts to nearby communities and infrastructure.Important InformationAll UpdatesMapsCurrent Fire RestrictionsBeehive Fire Closures
item.dc:creator: zbehrens

item.title: COPSF Willow Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-07-06
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Pike and San Isabel National Forest.
---
State: Colorado
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 39° 14 00 Longitude: 106° 26 25
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Willow Fire, located approximately 6 miles west of Leadville, CO, started on June 28, 2026. Southwest Area Team 4, lead by Incident Commander, Andrew Mandell, assumed command at 7:00 a.m. on June 30, 2026.For current closure and evacuation information, please follow Lake County Office of Emergency Management. We ask that the public respect the closures and evacuation area and avoid the area of Mt Massive and Hagerman Pass.
item.dc:creator: dsanchez

item.title: WASESS Chelan Hills

---

Latitude: 47° 47 58 Longitude: 119° 58 36
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: On 7/4/2026 @ 0029 a fire started and local resources began initial attack. Southeast Washington Type 3 Incident command took over command at 6:00 PM on 7/4/26.
item.dc:creator: dweymouth

item.title: NVWID Dutch Flat Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-07-06
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Winnemucca District.
---
State: Nevada
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 41° 4 43 Longitude: 117° 36 50
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: On the day the incident was reported, the fire grew rapidly as thunderstorms produced gusty, erratic winds. The north side of the fire is burning in the remote Hot Springs Mountain Range. The fire started on private land before spreading onto Bureau of Land Management-administered lands within the Winnemucca District.
item.dc:creator: nhalloran

item.title: IDNCF Shingle Creek
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-07-06
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Nez Perce - Clearwater National Forests.
---
State: Idaho
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 45° 21 18.828 Longitude: 116° 24 8.208
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Shingle Creek Fire started June 21. Idaho Department of Lands (IDL), the Forest Service, and multiple partners responded. Northern Rockies Team 1 assumed command June 25 at 0600. Command of the fire was returned to the Nez Perce-Clearwater National Forest July 2nd at 0600.The fire is currently in steep, rugged terrain, creating challenges for firefighters. As conditions become drier, fire activity may increase, but the work that firefighters have done so far has substantially reduced the risk of fire spreading. Continued patrol will be critical as the season progresses to ensure the fire stays within its footprint.
item.dc:creator: annahenderson

item.title: NVELD Parsnip Peak
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-07-05
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Ely District.
---
State: Nevada
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 38° 5 0 Longitude: 114° 17 35
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Parsnip Peak Fire is burning in the remote Parsnip Peak Wilderness Area and only accessible by helicopter. Fuel moisture levels are at critical levels.
item.dc:creator: nhalloran

Last updated: 2026-06-30

The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Ely District.
---

---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 37° 17 4 Longitude: 114° 37 49
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Kane Springs Fire is burning in short grass, brush, and timber, 17 Miles Southwest of Caliente, NV.

---

Incident Overview: The Log Road Fire was first reported 11:37am June 29th.
item.dc:creator: cduggin

item.title: MNSUF Long 2 Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-07-05
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Superior National Forest.
---
State: Minnesota
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 48° 3 17 Longitude: 90° 9 13
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: Mid-afternoon on July 2, 2026, the Long 2 Fire was detected during a routine fire patrol flight on the Gunflint Ranger District of the Superior National Forest within the Boundary Waters Canoe Area Wilderness (BWCAW). Firefighters were flown in by Beaver aircraft to begin direct suppression. The fire is smoldering and creeping in pine needle litter and brush. The fire has been determined to be lightning caused by recent thunderstorm activity earlier in the week.
item.dc:creator: ckolinski

item.title: UTWDD Cherry Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-07-04
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) West Desert District.
---
State: Utah
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 39° 51 17 Longitude: 112° 21 57
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Cherry Fire was reported at approximately 10:30 a.m. Friday, June 26, 2026, and grew rapidly under extreme fire weather. It was reported six miles southwest of Highway 36. The fire is burning primarily in grass, sagebrush, and scattered juniper. It made a significant run Friday, June 26th, extending approximately 11 miles from south to north in only a few hours. Great Basin Complex Incident Management Team 3, which was overseeing the nearby Iron Fire, took over command of the Cherry Fire on Saturday, June 27 at 6:00 a.m. Please rely on official sources for evacuation notices, road closure, shelter information, and re-entry decisions, including:Utah Fire Info: Utah Fire Info page on Facebook and XJuab County Sheriff’s Office Facebook pageTooele County Sheriff's Office Facebook pageEureka City alerts: Eureka City websiteTooele County: Emergency Management Alerts
item.dc:creator: liverson

item.title: UTUTS Iron
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-07-05
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Utah Division Forestry Fire State Lands.
---
State: Utah
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 39° 58 35 Longitude: 112° 11 51
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Iron Fire is 1 mile northwest of Eureka in Juab County, Utah. The fire was reported late on the evening of Friday, June 19. The fire quickly grew in size and was estimated to be 1,000+ acres by the following morning, burning into Juab, Utah, and Toole counties. Great Basin Complex Incident Management Team 3 assumed command of the Iron Fire at 6:00AM on June 22, 2026. On Saturday, June 27, the same incident management team took over command of the Cherry Fire at 6:00AM and will be managing both the Iron and Cherry Fires.Please rely on official sources for evacuation notices, road closures, shelter information, and re-entry decisions, including:Utah Fire Info: Utah Fire Info page on Facebook and XJuab County Sheriff’s Office Facebook pageTooele County Sheriff's Office Facebook pageEureka City alerts: Eureka City websiteTooele County: Emergency Management AlertsDaily Fire Fact Sheets and maps can be found on the News tab of this Inciweb site.
item.dc:creator: mdavis1

item.title: NMSNF McCauley Springs Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-07-04
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Santa Fe National Forest.
---
State: New Mexico
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 35° 49 38 Longitude: 106° 37 33.6
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The McCauley Springs Fire was detected Wednesday morning June 24, 2026, on the Jemez Ranger District. The fire transferred command back to the Santa Fe National Forest, Jemez Ranger district on Sunday July 5, 2026.
item.dc:creator: matthewgarcia

item.title: AZTNF Sycamore
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-07-02
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Tonto National Forest.
---
State: Arizona
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 33° 36 17 Longitude: 110° 45 32
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Sycamore Fire was discovered Monday, June 15, 2026, north of Globe on the Globe Ranger District. Firefighters are utilizing both direct extinguishment and indirect confinement methods as part of a full-suppression strategy. Ground resources are being utilized where the probability of success is highest due to the remote location, steep rugged terrain and poor access.
item.dc:creator: smoore3

---

---
Incident Overview: The Steamboat Fire was reported the afternoon of June 25, 2026 and declared contained on July 1, 2026.

item.title: UTPAP Dellenbaugh Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-06-22
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Grand Canyon - Parashant National Monument.
---
State: Utah
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 36° 4 30 Longitude: 113° 32 7.2
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Dellenbaugh Fire began the afternoon of Monday, June 15 in the Grand Canyon–Parashant National Monument south of Mount Dellenbaugh. The fire is now 100% contained.
item.dc:creator: jwonderly

item.title: IDBOF Poorman Prescribed Rx Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-06-30
---
The type of incident is Prescribed Fire and involves the following unit(s) Boise National Forest.
---
State: Idaho
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 44° 04 36.2 Longitude: 115° 53 34.8
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: Update: Poorman Prescribed Fire (June 30 at 4:00 p.m.)The area received 1.7 inches of rain over the past weekend along with below average temperatures. The warming and drying trend is expected to return Thursday with temperatures predicted to rise into the upper 80s and low 90s by the end of the week.After the wetting rain event, fire activity and smoke impacts remain minimal. Highway 17/Banks-Lowman Road remained free of debris over the weekend, but crews will continue to patrol for potential rollout. The Bear River Wildland Fire Module will be on the unit throughout the week and will also be available for Initial Attack response should the need arise.For questions about the Poorman Prescribed Fire, call 208-861-3465BackgroundThe Poorman Prescribed Fire is a 2,692‑acre fuels‑reduction project designed to lower the risk of high‑intensity wildfire near the communities of Crouch and Garden Valley. This area has been successfully treated with prescribed fire in the past, including the late 1990s and again from 2010–2012.This project is one of several active forest management efforts on the Boise National Forest to reduce wildfire risk and support firefighter safety. In spring 2026, seven prescribed burns were successfully implemented across the Boise National Forest.Ignitions for the Poorman Prescribed Fire took place over four days from late April through early May. Firefighters have worked daily to patrol the control line to reinforce it against any nearby fire activity that could possibly threaten it. The fire has been meeting treatment objectives, consuming pine needles, downed logs, brush, and some grasses. However, unusually dry weather in May has led to smoke lingering in the Garden Valley area longer than anticipated.Current SituationDue to ongoing dry conditions and continued smoke impacts, firefighters are taking additional steps to reduce fuels and shorten the duration of smoke in the area:• Perimeter work: Beginning June 2, firefighters widened the existing handline around the project boundary by carefully burning grasses along the edge. This work was completed on June 3 and further reduces the likelihood of fire moving outside the project area.• Interior burning: Firefighters will be burning remaining grasses inside the project area starting June 4, with work expected to continue for up to four days. This may temporarily increase smoke but will help reduce future smoke production and speed up the overall clean‑up period.Going ForwardThis InciWeb site will be updated regularly while the prescribed fire is being patrolled. For questions about the Poorman Prescribed Fire, call 208-861-3465
item.dc:creator: ahostad

item.title: COUPD Beehive Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-06-29
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Uncompahgre Field Office.
---
State: Colorado
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 38° 25 05.1 Longitude: 108° 53 18.2
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Beehive Fire ignited on June 8, 2026. The fire was started by a lightning storm in the area on the evening of June 7, 2026.
item.dc:creator: ckilbane

item.title: NMSNF Rio Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-06-27
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Santa Fe National Forest.
---
State: New Mexico
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 36° 2 29 Longitude: 106° 20 56
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Rio Fire is a lightning caused fire located on the Espanola Ranger District of the Santa Fe National Forest in Mesa De La Gallina three miles northeast of Chicoma Mountain. It was reported on June 16, 2026.
item.dc:creator: matthewgarcia

item.title: IDPDS Cherry Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-06-28
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Ponderosa Area Office.
---
State: Idaho
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 46° 49 37 Longitude: 116° 32 09
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Cherry Fire started on the evening of 7/14/2025. The Idaho Department of Lands (IDL) in cooperation with the  US Forest Service, Bennett Lumber, Clearwater Potlatch Timber Protective Association, Deary Rural Fire Department, Juliaetta Fire Department, and the Montana Department of Natural Resources and Conservation has successfully contained the fire as of 7/21/2025.   The cause of the fire is under investigation.
item.dc:creator: kmaddox

item.title: IDPDS Cherry Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-06-28
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Ponderosa Area Office.
---
State: Idaho
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 46° 49 37 Longitude: 116° 32 09
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Cherry Fire started on the evening of 7/14/2025. The Idaho Department of Lands (IDL) in cooperation with the  US Forest Service, Bennett Lumber, Clearwater Potlatch Timber Protective Association, Deary Rural Fire Department, Juliaetta Fire Department, and the Montana Department of Natural Resources and Conservation has successfully contained the fire as of 7/21/2025.   The cause of the fire is under investigation.
item.dc:creator: kmaddox

item.title: AZFTA Flat Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-06-28
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Fort Apache Agency.
---
State: Arizona
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 34° 15 30 Longitude: 110° 41 40
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Flat Fire is a lightning-caused wildfire located approximately 9 miles southwest of Heber, Arizona within Navajo Count. The fire is currently estimated at 134 acres and is 83% contained. The incident will transition on June 29th at 6:00am from the White Mountain Zone Type 3 team to a Type 4 organization.During the current operational period, fire activity remained minimal, consisting primarily of smoldering and creeping within the interior. Firefighters will continue mop up operations and perimeter patrols to secure containment lines and keep the fire within the established perimeter. Fuels consist primarily of medium logging slash, brush, and tall grasses.A Red Flag Warning remains in effect through Sunday evening, with dry breezy conditions and near-critical fire weather expected to continue into next week. Gusty winds may produce interior flare ups and localized spotting potential over the next 72 hours; however, no current threat to communities, infrastructure, or other values at risk has been identified.There has been no reported damage to structures or infrastructure, no civilian impacts. Approximately 192 personnel remain assigned to the incident in support of suppression and containment efforts.Operational priorities remained focused on patrolling and securing the fire perimeter, completing mop-up where needed, implementing suppression repair, and maintaining containment.
item.dc:creator: sjohnson2

item.title: NENBF South Fork Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-06-24
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Nebraska National Forest.
---
State: Nebraska
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 42° 39 33.0 Longitude: 103° 38 18.0
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The South Fork Fire ignited on June 9, 2026, in the Soldier Creek Wilderness area of the Nebraska National Forest’s Pine Ridge Ranger District near Crawford, Nebraska. Local responders, U.S. Forest Service crews and volunteer firefighting partners engaged the fire early as dry fuels, steep terrain and strong winds drove rapid spread. U.S. Highway 20 was closed by the Nebraska Department of Transportation as the fire jumped the highway. A red flag warning was in effect through Wednesday, June 10.By June 10, the fire had grown to nearly 9,000 acres and Nebraska’s Governor Pillen officially issued an emergency declaration for the South Fork Fire. It was fueled by rugged terrain, dry vegetation including timber and grassy undergrowth, and strong winds. On June 11 at 6 am the Rocky Mountain Complex Incident Management Team 2, under the command of Jay Mickey, assumed control of the fire.On June 12, containment increased to 7% even as the fire grew to about 28,067 acres. Resources including National Guard units, ground crews, dozers and aircraft focused on defending the towns of Crawford and the Fort Robinson State Park area. The Red Cross established community shelter options at the Chadron State College–Andrews Hall.By June 15, containment was at 55% and the fire had grown to 38,914 acres. 581 personnel were supporting the fire. Forecasters predicted that the week ahead would bring sustained winds anticipated between 23 and 30 miles per hour, and the possibility of gusts escalating to as high as 50 miles per hour. Nebraska’s Governor, Jim Pillen received an operational visit of the South Fork Fire on June 16. He visited ICP and was provided with a comprehensive briefing. He had the opportunity to communicate with members of his constituency as well as incident team members. Containment reached 80% on June 17, with the acres climbing to 39,057. Although conditions continued to change, fire activity was generally quiet. A NIROPS flight (a thermal imaging aircraft used to map active wildfires) was ordered after several areas of hot spots were discovered. Line is now in place around the entire fire perimeter. Crews are working to secure and mop up the containment line and will continue for several days. Suppression repair work is also u begin underway and will continue in the next few days. On June 20th the overall acreage of the South Fork Fire increased to 39,696 due to more accurate satellite mapping showing previously unseen grassy areas and containment increased to 90%.Rocky Mountain CIMT2 transfered command to the Nebraska Type 3 team on Sunday, June 21, 2026, at 6 p.m.  Please follow the Nebraska National Forest on Facebook and the Nebraska National Forest website's Alerts and Notices page for further updates.
item.dc:creator: cduggin

item.title: AZASD Rock Canyon Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-06-24
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Arizona Strip District Office.
---
State: Utah
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 36° 54 56.5 Longitude: 112° 12 11.5
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: Rock Canyon Fire was discovered on June 15, 2026, at 1:47 PM. The Color Country Type 3 Incident Management Team (IMT) will be transitioning to a local Type 4 incident management organization June 25, 2026. Fire Area Closures for both the Bureau of Land Management - Arizona Strip District and the U.S. Forest Service - Kaibab National Forest will be lifted effective 0600 on June 25, 2026. There will be no further updates on the incident here or on social media unless unforeseen activity warrants.
item.dc:creator: jvanbuskirk

item.title: MIHMF 2026 HuronManistee NFs SpringSummer Prescribed Fire Season
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-06-24
---
The type of incident is Prescribed Fire and involves the following unit(s) Huron-Manistee National Forest.
---
State: Michigan
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 44 ° 15 7 Longitude: 85 ° 24 4
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Huron-Manistee National Forests Fire and Aviation Management Unit is preparing for prescribed fire operations.Rx burns are planned events that are matched to predetermined weather/fuel parameters. We will evaluate weather conditions in the hours before a burn begins. If conditions warrant, scheduled prescribed fire activities may be canceled. Updates including cancellations, additions and corrections will be added to this post.Prescribed fires reduce risk to the public by removing hazardous vegetation to mitigate wildfires and prevent loss of property. They also help to re-establish fire’s positive role in local ecosystems, increase native plant regeneration and improve wildlife habitat and forest health. During active burning, smoke and flames may be visible from roads and in areas downwind of the burn site. Residents may experience smoke during the prescribed burns. When driving, slow down and turn on your headlights when you encounter smoke on the road. For more detailed information about air quality, go to AirNow online or download the app. Residents and motorists may also encounter roadblocks, specially trained fire personnel and heavy equipment, such as engines and dozers, performing Rx Fire operations. The planned prescribed fires listed below are organized by ranger district, county and township. Respective recreational trails and sites may close for public safety. Watch for warning signs along roads near all prescribed fire areas before and during burns. Stay informed about the scheduled prescribed fires through the forest website, social media channels, and InciWeb, the interagency incident information system. We will notify county emergency management officials when burning begins. If you wish to be notified about prescribed fires in your vicinity, contact your nearest District Ranger Office.Firefighters will monitor the burn area for days following the burn. Baldwin/White Cloud Ranger DistrictLake CountyChase Twp.Sears Tract Chase Rd. (84 Acres) (Map)       North of Hwy 10 NW, between State and S. Frank Smith Roads along the Pere Marquette State Trail. Peacock Twp.SE Peacock Block 3 (57 Acres) (Map)Northwest of Wolf Lake; north of Wolf Lake Drive and east of Powerline Road.Peacock Block 4 (13 Acres) (Map)     South of 4 Mile and east of N. Astor Roads.  SE Peacock Block 5 (15 Acres) (Map)South of 4 Mile and east of N. Astor Roads.  Pleasant Plains Twp.Baldwin Fuels Unit 6 Rx (658 Acres) (Map) Completed Sunday May 3, 2026North of 64th Street, between South Jenks Road and South Peacock Trail. Barren Plains Unit 1 (200 Acres) (Map) Completed Thursday April 30, 2026.     South of 76th Street and west of South Forman Road, north of the powerline.       Barren Plains Unit 3 (322 Acres) (Map) Completed Thursday April 30, 2026.     South of 76th Street and west of South Forman Road, south of the powerline.       Yates Twp.Chase Block 5 (289 Acres) (Map)     South of 88th Street and east of S. Queens Hwy.       Chase Block 6  (216 Acres) (Map) Active Saturday May 2, 2026.          South of 88th Street, between S. Queens and Kings Hwys. FR5099 and FR6191 will be impacted by temporary operations.   Chase Block 8 (57 Acres) (Map)          North of 88th Street and west of Spruce Road.         Chase Block 9 (482 Acres) (Map)      South of 88th Street, between Spruce and Eastman Roads, north of East 18 Mile Road. Idlewild Fuels Unit 2 (59 Acres)  (Map)  Completed Sunday May 3, 2026       North of E. 68th Street and east of S. Nelson Road.  Idlewild Fuels Unit 3 (29 Acres) (Map)  Completed Wednesday April 30, 2026.         East of S. Forman Road, between 72nd and E 76th Street. FR6792 may be impacted by temporary operations. Idlewild Fuels Unit 5  (113 Acres) (Map) Active Saturday May 2, 2026.  South of E. 76th Street and east of S. Forman Road. Idlewild Fuels Unit 6 (31 Acres) (Map) Completed Thursday April 30, 2026.     Southeast of E 76th Street, along FR 6792, which will be impacted by temporary operations.            Idlewild Fuels Unit 7 (197 Acres) (Map) Active Saturday May 2, 2026.           South of 72nd Street and west of Broadway Road.    Mason CountyEden Twp.Sippy Rd. Stickney and Sippy Roads, north of Whiskey Creek, east side of the North Branch of the Pentwater River.Newaygo CountyBrooks Twp.Bigelow Block 4 (58 Acres) (Map)North of 96th Street, between Spruce and Poplar Roads.     Coolbaugh Rx 8 (130 Acres) (Map)North of E 58th Street, and east of the Bigelow Creek along the North Country Trail (NCT). Forest Roads 8886, 8467, 8259 and 8988 will be impacted by temporary operations.Little River Rd. (408 Acres) (Map)    North of Little River Road, west of Six Mile Bridge Road. FR 8452 will be impacted by temporary operations.North Star/Custer Rd. Countyline and east of 6 Mile Bridge Roads, which may be impacted by temporary operations south of Little River Road.Mason County Freesoil Twp. Koenig/South Hoags  Night Hawk (Map); Completed Thursday May 14, 2026.  Southeast of Hoagues Lake between Forest Service Roads 8650 and 8539. Looney 105 (95 Acres) (Map) Planned for the week of May 17-23, 2026North of Hasenbank Road, near FR5037.Rail 78 (78 Acres) (Map) Active Sunday May 3, 2026.  East of the railway between South Countyline and East Hoague Roads.Stephens 98 (98 Acres) (Map) South of the intersection of East and South Countyline Roads, West along Stephens Road. Recreation in Mack Lake Area may be impacted by temporary operations.Mack Lake Fuel Break Middle (126 Acres) (Map) CompletedNorth of Curtisville Road, between Eggleston and Warbler (FR4147) Roads.Mack Lake North Fuel Break (21 Acres) (Map) Planned for  the Week of June 22 to 26, 2026Southwest of the intersection of Keeley (CR489) and Eggleton Roads; in the vicinity of the village of Mack Lake, which may be impacted by temporary operations.Mack McKinely Unit 6_9 (249 Acres) (Map)East of S. Mt. Tom Road (M-33), south and west of the ORV Trail on Forest Road 4457.Mack McKinley Unit 7 (125 Acres) (Map)South of East Hughes Lake near the intersection with Eggleston Roads.Six Mile Unit 3 (279 Acres) (Map) West of F32 and south of North Road.Wolf Creek (299 Acres) (Map)North of S. item.dc:creator: dabrabazon

item.title: NMGNF Bear Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-06-23
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Gila National Forest.
---
State: New Mexico
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 34° 2 47 Longitude: 108° 17 34
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: On June 9, 2026, aerial and ground firefighting resources began engaging in suppression actions on the Bear Fire, a wildfire located east of Mangus Mountain on the Quemado Ranger District of the Gila National Forest. The Gila Las Cruces Type 3 Incident Management Team, led by Incident Commander, Marcus Cornwell, assumed command of the Bear Fire on June 11, 2026.
item.dc:creator: rscavo

item.title: MIHIF 2026 Upper Peninsula Fire and Aviation Management Unit Planned Prescribed Fires East Zone
item.description:
Last updated:
---
The type of incident is Prescribed Fire and involves the following unit(s) Hiawatha National Forest.
---
State: Michigan
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 45° 52 39.9 Longitude: 84° 49 50.4
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Upper Peninsula Fire and Aviation Management Unit is planning prescribed fire operations this year, pending all required approvals. We estimate this work to continue this spring through the fall as weather conditions allow. Units that do not experience conducive conditions this season will be postponed. We use prescribed fires to help reduce overgrown vegetation to help protect local communities, infrastructure and natural resources from wildfires. Prescribed fires planned for the area include 10,082 acres in 44 units on the Hiawatha National Forest.Those areas may close to the public for several days for public safety. Watch for warning signs along roads near all prescribed fire areas before and during burns. Residents may experience smoke during the prescribed burns. For more detailed information about air quality, go to AirNow online or download the app. When driving, slow down and turn on your headlights when you encounter smoke on the road.We will evaluate weather conditions in the hours before a burn begins. If conditions warrant, scheduled prescribed fire activities may be canceled.If you wish to be notified about prescribed fires in your vicinity, contact your nearest Ranger District office.  An online map is also available.  Hiawatha National Forest East Zone Planned Prescribed Fire Projects   Unit 2, 201 acresUnit 3, 222 acresUnit 4, 351 acresUnit 5, 380 acresUnit 6, 185 acresUnit 7, 2948 acresCoaster, 134 acresDonnybrook North, 202 acresDonnybrook South, 363 acresBrevoort, 97 acresGala, 402 acresHigh Banks, 977 acresJonathan, 134 acresPaula Red, 254 acresRed Delicious, 289 acres
item.dc:creator: shanellesaunders

item.title: MIHIF 2026 Upper Peninsula Fire and Aviation Management Unit Planned Prescribed Fires West Zone
item.description:
Last updated:
---
The type of incident is Prescribed Fire and involves the following unit(s) Hiawatha National Forest.
---
State: Michigan
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 45° 56 15.7 Longitude: 86° 57 49.7
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Upper Peninsula Fire and Aviation Management Unit is planning prescribed fire operations this year, pending all required approvals. We estimate this work to continue this spring through the fall as weather conditions allow. Units that do not experience conducive conditions this season will be postponed. We use prescribed fires to help reduce overgrown vegetation to help protect local communities, infrastructure and natural resources from wildfires. Prescribed fires planned for the area include 10,082 acres in 44 units on the Hiawatha National Forest.Those areas may close to the public for several days for public safety. Watch for warning signs along roads near all prescribed fire areas before and during burns. Residents may experience smoke during the prescribed burns. For more detailed information about air quality, go to AirNow online or download the app. When driving, slow down and turn on your headlights when you encounter smoke on the road.We will evaluate weather conditions in the hours before a burn begins. If conditions warrant, scheduled prescribed fire activities may be canceled.If you wish to be notified about prescribed fires in your vicinity, contact your nearest Ranger District office.  An online map is also available.  Hiawatha National Forest West Zone Planned Prescribed Fire ProjectsAu Train, 202 acresStueben Lake 1, 100 acresTombolo, 322 acresHighbridge #1, 129 acresHighbridge #4, 31 acres8 Mile #3 & 4, 187 acres8 Mile #5, 44 acresEast Lake #1, 577 acresEast lake #2, 65 acresEast Lake #3 & 4, 73 acresIndian River #1, 53 acresIndian River #2, 88 acresIndian River #3, 7 acresEast Point #3, 7 acresEast Point #4, 9 acresEast Point #5, 30 acresJack Pine Lodge, 54 acresBeaton Boot PU 16, 31 acresMuleshoe PU 20, 15 acresPine Plains #2, 18 acresPine Plains #5, 81 acresPine Plains #6, 79 acresPine Plains #7, 87 acresDunklee, 183 acresWest Branch, 250 acresDutch Mill #2, 96 acresSavanna #1, 17 acresSavanna #2, 18 acresStonington Farm Field, 32 acres
item.dc:creator: shanellesaunders

item.title: MIOTF 2026 Upper Peninsula Fire and Aviation Management Unit Planned Prescribed Fires
item.description:
Last updated:
---
The type of incident is Prescribed Fire and involves the following unit(s) Ottawa National Forest.
---
State: Michigan
---

Latitude: 46° 15 43.8 Longitude: 89° 10 30.4

---
Incident Overview: The Upper Peninsula Fire and Aviation Management Unit is planning prescribed fire operations this year, pending all required approvals. We estimate this work to continue this spring through the fall as weather conditions allow. Units that do not experience conducive conditions this season will be postponed. We use prescribed fires to help reduce overgrown vegetation to help protect local communities, infrastructure and natural resources from wildfires.Prescribed fires planned for the area include 1,143 acres in 14 units on the Ottawa National Forest. Those areas may close to the public for several days for public safety. Watch for warning signs along roads near all prescribed fire areas before and during burns. Residents may experience smoke during the prescribed burns. For more detailed information about air quality, go to AirNow online or download the app. When driving, slow down and turn on your headlights when you encounter smoke on the road.We will evaluate weather conditions in the hours before a burn begins. If conditions warrant, scheduled prescribed fire activities may be canceled.If you wish to be notified about prescribed fires in your vicinity, contact your nearest Ranger District office. An online map is also available.    Ottawa National Forest Planned Prescribed Fire Projects Iron Pike, 15 acres Lucky Pond, 181 acres Sleeper Cell East, 155 acres Pinto, 27 acres Cooks, 93 acres Charming, 148 acres Sleeper Cell West, 25 acresAho 14, 45 acres Bluff, 35 acres 1300 Railroad, 39 acres 6950 Rx, uA & B, 17 acres Watersmeet Office, 6 acres Blue Prairie, 334 acres Soo Hemlock, 23 acres

item.title: MTGNF East Side Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-06-22
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Custer Gallatin National Forest.
---
State: Montana
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 45° 5 31 Longitude: 109° 18 51
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The East Side Fire ignited on April 20, 2026.  All evacuation orders and warnings for the East Side Fire were lifted Wednesday, April 22.
item.dc:creator: mdemeyer

item.title: WAYAA 4170 Tule Rd
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-06-21
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Yakama Agency.
---
State: Washington
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 46° 17 47 Longitude: 120° 13 17
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The 4170 Tule Rd Fire began on June 14, 2026. Driven by high winds it quickly grew to over 20,000 acres. Local resources worked day and night to halt fire growth, which burned in light, flashy, fuels. On June 17, Northwest Team 2 assumed command of the fire. Over the next few days firefighters completed establishing direct and indirect line around the fire perimeter and quickly built containment across most of the fire. As of June 21, crews are mopping up, patrolling the perimeter, assessing for resource damage, and conducting backhaul. A Burned Area Emergency Response (BAER) Team will be responding to the 4170 Tule Rd Fire to support the post-fire emergency response. BAER teams specialize in identifying the repair and rehabilitation needs of fire affected areas, caused by the fire itself and the suppression activities. These teams often include expertise in hydrology, soils, biology, botany, fisheries, and archeology and will work to provide recommendations to the local unit for short-term and long-term emergency stabilization actions to repair and improve the land.
item.dc:creator: blawatch

item.title: WASPD Roza Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-06-20
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Spokane District.
---
State: Washington
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 46° 44 24 Longitude: 120° 24 36
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Roza Fire began on June 16, 2026. The fuels are light and flashy and grew rapidly due to high winds and hot temperatures. Northwest Team 2 worked closely with the local Type 3 organization during transition and assumed command of the fire on June 17 at 7:00 AM. Over the next few days, firefighters established direct line around the fire perimeter and quickly built containment across most of the fire. Northwest Team 2 transferred command of the fire back to the local unit on June 21 at 7:00 AM. At the time of the transfer, crews were mopping up, patrolling the perimeter, assessing for resource damage and working on suppression repair.
item.dc:creator: blawatch

item.title: AZTNF Billy Fire
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-06-19
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Tonto National Forest.
---
State: Arizona
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 33° 51 35 Longitude: 110° 54 22
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: This is the final update of the Type 3 team as they transfer command of the fire back to the local district as of tomorrow morning. 64 personnel remain assigned to the Billy Fire.Unless there are significant changes in fire activity, this will be the last daily update of the Billy Fire.-The Billy Fire is a lightning-caused wildfire on the Tonto National Forest, 15 miles south of Young, Arizona. Firefighters are taking a full suppression strategy on the fire situated on the east side of the Sierra Ancha Mountains.
item.dc:creator: smoore3

item.title: CACNP Santa Rosa Island Burned Area Emergency Response BAER
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-06-16
---
The type of incident is Burned Area Emergency Response and involves the following unit(s) Channel Islands National Park.
---
State: California
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 33° 55 2 Longitude: 120° 5 10
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: Current Conditions:On 6/16, the Burned Area Emergency Response (BAER) team presented their recommendations and funding specifications to Channel Islands National Park staff. These emergency stabilization recommendations will will help park staff prioritize actions needed to reduce risks including erosion, flooding, hazardous trees, and impacts to sensitive resources. Using information collected during their evaluations, the team identified immediate threats to life, safety, property, and critical natural and cultural resources that may be caused by the fire. While the park will implement time-sensitive projects over the summer, many projects will last for years, as the island recovers from the fire. Closures:Santa Rosa Island is closed to all day and overnight use through at least June 30, 2026, due to the wildfire. All Water Canyon Campground reservations have been cancelled through this date and visitors have been notified by email. Campground reservation holders with bookings through August 14, 2026, have also been informed that the wildfire may affect their stay. If additional cancellations are needed, they will receive follow-up emails.Backcountry camping within the southeastern quadrant of Santa Rosa Island will remain closed for the remainder of the 2026 recreation season.
item.dc:creator: atravaglio

item.title: WANES Snake River
item.description:
Last updated: 2026-06-14
---
The type of incident is Wildfire and involves the following unit(s) Northeast Region-Department of Natural Resources.
---
State: Washington
---
Coordinates:
Latitude: 46° 25 56 Longitude: 117° 11 28
---
NOTE: All fire perimeters and points are approximations.
---
Incident Overview: The Snake River Fire was reported at 5:55 PM on June 12th, along the Wawawai Road, approximately 4 miles west of Clarkston.  The fire quickly progressed north in flashy fuels (grasses and sagebrush) and in steep, rocky terrain, threatening rangeland, powerlines, communication infrastructure, rail infrastructure, and residences as Moses community.  Crews from Whitman County Fire Districts 14 and 12, Asotin County, DNR, and the nearby cities of Lewiston, Clarkson and Asotin engaged the fire, with the aid of a type 1 and type 2 helicopters.  State mobilization was authorized at 9:00PM on June 12th, coordinating additional district crews to respond to the fire. Northeast Washington Interagency Incident Management Team #1 has taken command of the fire as of 4:00PM on June 13th.
item.dc:creator: cnikkola

item.dc:creator: rshaffer
